ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Apperances: Mark Gullotta for Petitioner Donna Heuman.

Mr. Gullotta reported the ages of the minors are 7, 12 and 13 years old, that the Wells Fargo account is in Georgia and the it is not FDIC insured. The Court raised the issue that is appears that all the money is going into the same account and this may cause problems at the time for distribution. Mr. Gullotta stated that he believed that the accounts were all separate but he would have to investigate further and that this is a blocked account that requires withdraws are allowed only at the minors turning 25 years old or with a court order.

The Court stated that its' main concern is preservation of the funds and question what will the money be invested in. Mr. Gullotta requested that the order reflect that that account be FDIC insured and moved to California. The Court questioned if the account should be in the name of the minors father. Mr. Gullotta stated that this is a blocked account and should be in the name of the minors. The Court takes the accounting petition regarding final petition under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
